name: Hypothalamic stem-cell exosomes slowed mouse aging phenotypes
summary: Hypothalamic stem/progenitor-cell support and secreted exosomes slowed aging-like phenotypes in mice, while cell loss accelerated aging-like changes.
statement: In mice, hypothalamic stem/progenitor-cell ablation accelerated aging-like physiological changes or shortened lifespan, while hypothalamic stem/progenitor-cell implantation and central exosome treatment were reported to retard aging and extend or preserve aging-related function.
population_or_model: Mid-aged and aged mouse hypothalamic stem/progenitor-cell and exosome models
time_horizon: Months-long physiology and lifespan follow-up in mouse models
quantitative_note: Figure-level cohorts varied by assay; this finding is treated as preclinical support for hypothalamic secretory signaling rather than a clinical intervention.
caveats: The interventions were central mouse cell or exosome manipulations.
The active exosomal miRNA set and delivery strategy are not established for humans.
This supports neuroendocrine control of aging speed but not a deployable human therapy.
